identity.title: Under the Elevated
identity.styleSentence: A slow-burning (83 BPM) dark NYC R&B track featuring a bass-heavy boom-bap drum pattern without modern 808s, layered with dusty vinyl crackle and subway rumble FX; the arrangement blends cinematic noir strings (cello, viola), smoky pads, deep live kicks, rimshots, and raw piano to support a smooth yet wounded male vocal that shifts from soft vulnerability to desperate power.
identity.vocalStyle: Smooth vulnerable male R&B with melodic hip-hop phrasing; female whisper-singing representing addiction (seductive, gothic); spoken word narrator.

lyrics.text:
[Intro - Narrator, subway rumble]
This is for the ones nobody checks on
because they look like they got it together.
Bills paid. Kids fed. Smile ready.
But somewhere between Queens and Brooklyn,
under the elevated,
somebody is asking:
“Is this really addiction?”

[Female - whisper]
You work hard.
You deserve peace.
Come here...

[Verse 1 - Male, smooth R&B]
Queens Boulevard got my promises,
Brooklyn nights got my shame.
I keep showing up like I’m solid,
but I keep coming home the same.

Rent paid, lights on, fridge full,
everybody thinks I’m blessed.
I keep my body right, keep my money good,
still got smoke inside my chest.

Corner store glowing like a chapel,
midnight glass behind the door.
I tell myself I’m only looking,
but my hand already knows.

[Female - sung whisper]
Long day.
Don’t make it deep.
One little flame.
One little drink.
One little sleep.

[Pre-Chorus - Male]
And it don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like I’m still in control.
Like I could stop when I’m ready,
like I could leave it alone.

I quit three days just to prove it,
then came back like I won.
But that ain’t freedom,
that’s just losing with a different kind of gun.

[Narrator]
The cage don’t always rattle.
Sometimes it sings.

[Chorus - Male]
I’m chasing the dragon under the elevated,
trying to catch what don’t come back no more.
First time flew, now I’m stuck here waiting
with my heart face down on the floor.

It don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow.
Feels like one more night,
feels like borrowed sorrow.

I wanna quit,
but I want that fire.
I want the truth,
but I love the liar.

And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Verse 2 - Male, rhythmic R&B]
On the J train with my jaw tight,
on the 7 with my head low.
Every ad says buy your freedom,
every craving says don’t let go.

I got side dreams, side gigs,
plans folded in my notes.
But I keep feeding little habits
that keep standing on my throat.

I say “next week” like a prayer,
say “last time” like a song.
Say “I’m good” when I’m lying,
say “I’m fine” when I’m gone.

[Female - addiction POV]
Gone?
Baby, you’re here.
I’m the only thing clear
when the city gets heavy.
I disappear your fear.

You call me poison,
but I call you mine.
You call it weakness,
I call it time.

[Male]
Don’t call me yours.

[Female]
Then why do you answer?

[Narrator]
Addiction don’t always kick the door in.
Sometimes it uses your own voice.
Sometimes it says,
“You earned this.”

[Pre-Chorus 2 - Male]
At the counter with the music low,
card in my hand, heart in my throat.
I ask myself, “Do I need this?”
then laugh like it’s some kind of joke.

What else could I do with this money?
What else could I do with this pain?
What man keeps building a future
then burning the bridge in the rain?

[Chorus - Duet]
[Male]
I’m chasing the dragon under the elevated,
trying to catch what don’t come back no more.

[Female]
I can make it feel close.
I can open that door.

[Male]
It don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow.
Feels like one more night,
feels like borrowed sorrow.

[Female]
Borrow it, baby.
You can pay it later.

[Both]
And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Bridge - Narrator]
To the one saying “I can stop”
but never really stops:
this is the sign.
Not shame.
Not judgment.
Come back home.

[Bridge - Male]
Do I really need this?
Do I really want this?
Am I really free
if I keep running from the honest?

Do I want to stop,
or do I want to hide?
Do I want my life back,
or another alibi?

[Female - soft, dangerous]
Nobody knows you like I know you.
Nobody holds you like I hold you.

[Male - breaking]
That’s not love.
That’s not peace.
That’s you taking little pieces out of me.

I don’t need to hit bottom
just to prove the bottom’s real.
I don’t need another bottle
just to tell me how to feel.

[Breakdown - Male]
Help me.
Hear me.
I’m still in here.
Don’t fear me.

I look alright,
but I’m not okay.
I keep surviving,
but I wanna change.

Call me.
Hold me.
Don’t shame me.
Just know me.

I need love.
I need truth.
I need somebody
to pull me through.

[Female - tempting]
One more night.
One more flame.
One more pour.

[Final Chorus - Male]
I’m not chasing the dragon under the elevated,
not tonight, not like before.
First time’s gone and I’m done here waiting
for a ghost to give me more.

It did feel like addiction
when I finally saw the chain.
It did feel like a prison
when I called it by its name.

I wanna live
more than I want that fire.
I want the truth
more than I want the liar.

And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Final Duet]
[Female]
You’ll miss me by morning.

[Male]
Maybe I will.

[Female]
You’ll call me when it hurts.

[Male]
I’ll call someone real.

[Female]
You’ll come back to me.

[Male]
Not this time.

[Outro - Narrator]
For anybody fighting quiet:
you are not the thing you’re fighting.
Cry out.
Reach out.
Come back.
The night is loud,
but you are louder.

[Male - whisper]
Help me.
I’m ready.

[Female - fading]
One more...

[Male]
No.

analysis.summary: A gritty cinematic exploration of addiction personified as a seductive force in late-night NYC, where the narrator struggles between maintaining appearances and confronting the 'dragon' beneath their facade.
themes: The Mask of Success vs. Internal Decay

keywords.imageryPrompt: Thick grey haze swirling around a dimly lit corner store window, obscuring the street behind it like a ghostly veil.
A shadowy serpentine silhouette coiled beneath the concrete underbelly of an elevated train track, glowing with faint emerald eyes in total darkness.
Tattered paper wings fluttering uselessly above a grimy subway grate, unable to escape the weight of heavy rain falling on concrete below.
Rusted iron links wrapped around an ankle resting against a cold brick wall in a narrow alleyway, casting long sharp shadows at midnight.
A single beam of harsh white light cutting through dense smoke to reveal cracked pavement and raw, unvarnished emotion on a face.
A distorted mirror reflecting a smiling figure while the glass cracks behind them with spiderweb fractures filled with dark liquid.

dropMap.hint: cuts → blackout/cut-mode moments; risers → supernova build-ups (stemSense activeCut/activeRiser fire these automatically)
